file.content:
-- Constellations feature: user-created links between stars in the Universe view

-- 1. Create constellations table
CREATE TABLE constellations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    star_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    star_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    label TEXT DEFAULT '',
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'accepted', 'declined')),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT different_users CHECK (user_id_a != user_id_b),
    CONSTRAINT different_stars CHECK (star_id_a != star_id_b),
    CONSTRAINT unique_star_pair UNIQUE (star_id_a, star_id_b)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_a ON constellations(star_id_a);
CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_b ON constellations(star_id_b);
CREATE INDEX idx_const_status ON constellations(status);

-- 2. Add constellation_id to notifications table
ALTER TABLE notifications
ADD COLUMN constellation_id UUID REFERENCES constellations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE DEFAULT NULL;

-- 3. RPC for viewport constellation fetch
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_viewport_constellations(UUID[]);
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_viewport_constellations(post_ids UUID[])
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID, star_id_a UUID, star_id_b UUID,
    user_id_a UUID, user_id_b UUID,
    label TEXT, status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    a_universe_x DOUBLE PRECISION, a_universe_y DOUBLE PRECISION,
    b_universe_x DOUBLE PRECISION, b_universe_y DOUBLE PRECISION
) AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT c.id, c.star_id_a, c.star_id_b, c.user_id_a, c.user_id_b,
           c.label, c.status, c.created_at,
           ja.universe_x AS a_universe_x, ja.universe_y AS a_universe_y,
           jb.universe_x AS b_universe_x, jb.universe_y AS b_universe_y
    FROM constellations c
    JOIN journals ja ON ja.id = c.star_id_a
    JOIN journals jb ON jb.id = c.star_id_b
    WHERE c.status IN ('accepted', 'pending')
      AND (c.star_id_a = ANY(post_ids) OR c.star_id_b = ANY(post_ids));
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE;

content.text:
All three constellation enhancements are implemented across 6 files:

### Feature 1: Star Ring Indicators
- **`StarGroup.jsx`** — Accepts `constellationColor` prop, renders a dashed `Circle` ring at `r * 2.8` with 35% opacity between the outer atmosphere and inner glow
- **`InteractiveLayer.jsx`** — Accepts `constellationStarColors` prop, looks up each post's color and passes it to `StarGroup`
- **`Universe.jsx`** — New `constellationStarColors` `useMemo` maps star IDs to their chain color, passed to `<InteractiveLayer>`

### Feature 2: Ethereal Glow Lines
- **`ConstellationLayer.jsx`** — Full rewrite using a single `<Shape>` with `sceneFunc` Canvas2D rendering:
  - **Accepted lines**: 3 layers — wide underglow (8px, shadow blur 20), medium body (3px, shadow blur 8), thin animated dashed core (1.2px, `lineDashOffset` driven by `performance.now()`)
  - **Pending lines**: Single faint dashed line (1px, opacity 0.08)
  - Animation uses `requestAnimationFrame` + `batchDraw()` pattern
  - Labels remain as Konva `<Text>` nodes with zoom correction

### Feature 3: Constellation Travel Buttons
- **`constellations.sql`** — Added `a_title TEXT, b_title TEXT` to RPC return type, selecting `ja.title` / `jb.title` from already-joined journals
- **`Universe.jsx`** — `getConstellationLinksForPost` callback finds all constellation links for a selected star; `handleConstellationTravel` flies to the target using `finalStarPositions` with fallback to raw RPC coordinates
- **`Universe.module.css`** — Purple-accented `.constellationBtn` (`#b05fff`) to distinguish from blue wormhole buttons

**Note:** You'll need to run the updated SQL (`DROP FUNCTION` + `CREATE`) on your Supabase database to add the `a_title`/`b_title` columns to the RPC return.

-- Constellations feature: user-created links between stars in the Universe view

-- 1. Create constellations table
CREATE TABLE constellations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    star_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    star_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    label TEXT DEFAULT '',
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'accepted', 'declined')),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT different_users CHECK (user_id_a != user_id_b),
    CONSTRAINT different_stars CHECK (star_id_a != star_id_b),
    CONSTRAINT unique_star_pair UNIQUE (star_id_a, star_id_b)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_a ON constellations(star_id_a);
CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_b ON constellations(star_id_b);
CREATE INDEX idx_const_status ON constellations(status);

-- 2. Add constellation_id to notifications table
ALTER TABLE notifications
ADD COLUMN constellation_id UUID REFERENCES constellations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE DEFAULT NULL;

-- 3. RPC for viewport constellation fetch
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_viewport_constellations(UUID[]);
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_viewport_constellations(post_ids UUID[])
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID, star_id_a UUID, star_id_b UUID,
    user_id_a UUID, user_id_b UUID,
    label TEXT, status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    a_universe_x DOUBLE PRECISION, a_universe_y DOUBLE PRECISION,
    b_universe_x DOUBLE PRECISION, b_universe_y DOUBLE PRECISION,
    a_title TEXT, b_title TEXT
) AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT c.id, c.star_id_a, c.star_id_b, c.user_id_a, c.user_id_b,
           c.label, c.status, c.created_at,
           ja.universe_x AS a_universe_x, ja.universe_y AS a_universe_y,
           jb.universe_x AS b_universe_x, jb.universe_y AS b_universe_y,
           ja.title AS a_title, jb.title AS b_title
    FROM constellations c
    JOIN journals ja ON ja.id = c.star_id_a
    JOIN journals jb ON jb.id = c.star_id_b
    WHERE c.status IN ('accepted', 'pending')
      AND (c.star_id_a = ANY(post_ids) OR c.star_id_b = ANY(post_ids));
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE;

-- Constellations feature: user-created links between stars in the Universe view

-- 1. Create constellations table
CREATE TABLE constellations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    star_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    star_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    label TEXT DEFAULT '',
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'accepted', 'declined')),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT different_users CHECK (user_id_a != user_id_b),
    CONSTRAINT different_stars CHECK (star_id_a != star_id_b),
    CONSTRAINT unique_star_pair UNIQUE (star_id_a, star_id_b)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_a ON constellations(star_id_a);
CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_b ON constellations(star_id_b);
CREATE INDEX idx_const_status ON constellations(status);

-- 2. Add constellation_id to notifications table
ALTER TABLE notifications
ADD COLUMN constellation_id UUID REFERENCES constellations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE DEFAULT NULL;

-- 3. RPC for viewport constellation fetch
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_viewport_constellations(UUID[]);
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_viewport_constellations(post_ids UUID[])
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID, star_id_a UUID, star_id_b UUID,
    user_id_a UUID, user_id_b UUID,
    label TEXT, status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    a_universe_x DOUBLE PRECISION, a_universe_y DOUBLE PRECISION,
    b_universe_x DOUBLE PRECISION, b_universe_y DOUBLE PRECISION,
    a_title TEXT, b_title TEXT
) AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT c.id, c.star_id_a, c.star_id_b, c.user_id_a, c.user_id_b,
           c.label, c.status, c.created_at,
           ja.universe_x AS a_universe_x, ja.universe_y AS a_universe_y,
           jb.universe_x AS b_universe_x, jb.universe_y AS b_universe_y,
           ja.title::TEXT AS a_title, jb.title::TEXT AS b_title
    FROM constellations c
    JOIN journals ja ON ja.id = c.star_id_a
    JOIN journals jb ON jb.id = c.star_id_b
    WHERE c.status IN ('accepted', 'pending')
      AND (c.star_id_a = ANY(post_ids) OR c.star_id_b = ANY(post_ids));
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE;

1→# Constellation Enhancements: Star Indicators, Glow Lines, Travel Buttons
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Three enhancements to the constellation system:
     6→1. Stars in constellations have no visual indicator — users can't tell which stars are linked
     7→2. Constellation lines look like simple dashed lines, not like ethereal night-sky constellations
     8→3. You can only "wormhole" to semantically-linked stars, but not to constellation-linked stars
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## Feature 1: Constellation Ring Indicator on Stars
    13→
    14→Add a subtle dashed ring around stars that participate in accepted constellations, colored by their chain color.
    15→

    17→
    18→New `useMemo` after `chainColors` (~line 268):
    19→```js
    20→const constellationStarColors = useMemo(() => {
    21→    const map = new Map();
    22→    for (const c of acceptedConstellations) {
    23→        const color = chainColors.get(c.id);
    24→        if (!color) continue;
    25→        if (!map.has(c.star_id_a)) map.set(c.star_id_a, color);
    26→        if (!map.has(c.star_id_b)) map.set(c.star_id_b, color);
    27→    }
    28→    return map;
    29→}, [acceptedConstellations, chainColors]);
    30→```
    31→
    32→Pass `constellationStarColors` to `<InteractiveLayer>`.
    33→
    34→### 1.2 InteractiveLayer.jsx — thread to StarGroup
    35→
    36→- Accept `constellationStarColors` prop
    37→- In render: `const constellationColor = constellationStarColors?.get(post.id) || null;`
    38→- Pass `constellationColor` to `<StarGroup>`
    39→
    40→### 1.3 StarGroup.jsx — render ring
    41→
    42→Accept `constellationColor` prop. Add between outer atmosphere and inner glow:

    45→    <Circle
    46→        radius={r * 2.8}

    48→        strokeWidth={1.2}
    49→        opacity={0.35}
    50→        dash={[4, 3]}
    51→        perfectDrawEnabled={false}
    52→        listening={false}
    53→    />
    54→)}
    55→```
    56→
    57→---
    58→
    59→## Feature 2: Ethereal Constellation Glow Lines
    60→
    61→Replace current Konva `Line` components with a single Canvas2D `Shape` using triple-layer glow rendering.
    62→
    63→### 2.1 ConstellationLayer.jsx — full rewrite
    64→
    65→Replace the current multi-Line approach with a `Shape` using `sceneFunc`:
    66→
    67→**Accepted line draw function (3 layers per line):**
    68→- Layer 1: Wide underglow — solid, `strokeWidth: 8`, opacity `0.06`, `shadowBlur: 20`, `shadowColor` at `0.3` opacity
    69→- Layer 2: Medium body — solid, `strokeWidth: 3`, opacity `0.12`, `shadowBlur: 8`
    70→- Layer 3: Thin animated core — dashed `[14, 10]`, `strokeWidth: 1.2`, opacity `0.45`, animated `lineDashOffset` at `time * 0.04`
    71→
    72→**Pending lines:** Simple single-layer, `strokeWidth: 1`, dashed `[5, 10]`, opacity `0.08`
    73→
    74→**Animation:** Single `shapeRef` + `animRef.current.time` + `requestAnimationFrame` calling `batchDraw()` (same pattern as WormholeLayer)
    75→
    76→**Labels:** Keep as Konva `<Text>` nodes (need `scaleX/scaleY` zoom correction)
    77→
    78→---
    79→
    80→## Feature 3: Constellation Travel Buttons in Preview Panel
    81→
    82→### 3.1 SQL RPC — add star titles
    83→
    84→`server/sql/constellations.sql` — add `a_title TEXT, b_title TEXT` to return type:
    85→```sql
    86→DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_viewport_constellations(UUID[]);
    87→-- Add ja.title AS a_title, jb.title AS b_title to SELECT

    95→const getConstellationLinksForPost = useCallback((postId) => {
    96→    if (!postId || acceptedConstellations.length === 0) return [];
    97→    const links = [];
    98→    for (const c of acceptedConstellations) {
    99→        if (c.star_id_a === postId) {
   100→            links.push({ constellation: c, targetStarId: c.star_id_b, targetTitle: c.b_title || 'Unnamed Star', label: c.label });
   101→        } else if (c.star_id_b === postId) {
   102→            links.push({ constellation: c, targetStarId: c.star_id_a, targetTitle: c.a_title || 'Unnamed Star', label: c.label });

   153→2. **Glow lines**: Accepted constellation lines render with soft shadowBlur glow + animated dash core; look ethereal not mechanical

## Summary Report: Constellation Focus Feature Implementation

Based on my complete review of the five files, here are the key details for implementing the "constellation focus" feature:

---

### 1. **ConstellationLayer.jsx** (Lines 1-167)

**Current Structure:**
- Uses Canvas2D via `Shape` component with `sceneFunc` to render constellation lines
- **Line 68**: `listening={false}` is set on the Layer
- **Line 137**: `listening={false}` is also set on the Shape itself (double protection for non-interactivity)
- **Lines 69-138**: The Shape renders all constellation links via canvas context
- **Key detail**: `Line` from react-konva is **NOT imported** — it's using native Canvas2D drawing instead

**Line Rendering Pattern:**
- Pending links (lines 77-89): Simple dashed line with low opacity
- Accepted links (lines 92-134): Triple-layer glow system:
  - Layer 1 (wide underglow): 8px width, high shadowBlur (20)
  - Layer 2 (medium body): 3px width, medium shadowBlur (8)
  - Layer 3 (thin animated core): 1.2px dashed line with `lineDashOffset` animation (line 128: `time * 0.04`)

**Animation Setup:**
- Uses `requestAnimationFrame` to update `animRef.current.time` (line 44)
- Only animates if there are accepted constellations (line 54)

---

### 2. **StarGroup.jsx** (Lines 1-145)

**Current Props:**
```javascript
{ x, y, radius, color, title, showTitle, isPulsing, onClick, onPointerDown, constellationColor }
```

**Glow/Atmosphere Pattern:**
- **Lines 65-74**: Outer atmosphere — shadow glow (radius × 3, shadowBlur = r×5)
- **Lines 76-86**: Constellation membership ring — dashed circle if `constellationColor` prop provided
- **Lines 88-101**: Inner glow — radial gradient with 3 color stops
- **Lines 103-124**: Bright core — white center with shadow

**isPulsing Animation (Lines 11-56):**
- Two-phase tween using Konva.Tween:
  - Phase 1: Scale 1 → 1.8 over 0.35s with ElasticEaseOut (lines 23-29)
  - Phase 2: Scale 1.8 → 1 over 0.25s with EaseOut (lines 31-37)
- Automatically cleans up tweens on unmount

**Event Handlers:**
- `onClick` triggers on the bright core circle (line 111)
- `onPointerDown` handles mouse/touch for dragging (lines 113-123)

---

### 3. **InteractiveLayer.jsx** (Lines 1-229)

**Current Props Accepted:**
```javascript
{
  layerProps, posts, galaxies, zoom, showTitles, pulsingId,
  onStarClick, onGalaxyClick,
  galaxyPositions, starPullOffsets, similarPairs, galaxyEdges,
  activePostIds, useEmbeddingBridges,
  camera, stageSize,
  computedStarPositions, constellationStarColors
}
```

**Stars Rendering Pattern (Lines 193-221):**
- Viewport culling applied (lines 109-117)
- For each visible post, renders `StarGroup` with:
  - Computed position from `computedStarPositions` map
  - Color from `getStarColor(userId)`
  - `constellationColor` from `constellationStarColors?.get(post.id)` — used to show membership ring
  - `isPulsing={pulsingId === post.id}` for the elastic pulse effect

**Conditional Rendering:**
- Semantic bridges rendered as canvas Shape (lines 143-174) if `bridgePairs.length > 0`
- Galaxy spirals rendered as Shape (lines 176-180)
- Galaxy labels rendered if `galaxies?.map()` has data (lines 182-191)

---

### 4. **Universe.jsx** (Lines 1-903)

**State Management (Lines 47-58):**
```javascript
const [selectedPost, setSelectedPost] = useState(null);
const [selectedGalaxy, setSelectedGalaxy] = useState(null);
const [pulsingId, setPulsingId] = useState(null);
const [isWarping, setIsWarping] = useState(false);
const [constellations, setConstellations] = useState([]);
```

**Selected Post Preview Panel JSX (Lines 844-898):**
- Positioned bottom-right (line 845)
- Includes close button, author info, title, snippet, likes count
- **Action buttons section (lines 873-896)**:
  - Read button (line 874)
  - Wormhole button if applicable (lines 877-886)
  - Constellation travel buttons (lines 887-895) — loops through `getConstellationLinksForPost()`

**ESC Key Handler (Lines 629-637):**
```javascript
useEffect(() => {
    if (!isLinkMode) return;
    const handleKeyDown = (e) => {
        if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelLinkMode();
    };
    window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);
    return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);
}, [isLinkMode, cancelLinkMode]);
```

**Warp Overlay Pattern (Line 798):**
```jsx
{isWarping && <div className={styles.warpOverlay} />}
```
- Conditionally rendered during wormhole travel
- CSS animation handles fade in/out (lines 639-644 in CSS file)

**Props Passed to Constellation/Interactive Layers (Lines 766-795):**
```jsx
<ConstellationLayer
    layerProps={getLayerProps(1.0)}
    constellations={constellations}
    computedStarPositions={finalStarPositions}  // Shared hoisted positions
    zoom={zoom}
    chainColors={chainColors}
    userId={userId}
/>
<InteractiveLayer
    layerProps={getLayerProps(1.0)}
    posts={postArray}
    ...
    computedStarPositions={finalStarPositions}  // Same hoisted positions
    constellationStarColors={constellationStarColors}
/>
```

**Key Hoisted Positions (Lines 213-234):**
- `computedStarPositions` is computed once per frame and shared across all layers
- `finalStarPositions` includes constellation pull offsets applied (lines 248-262)

---

### 5. **Universe.module.css** (Lines 1-715)

**Warp Overlay Style (Lines 625-644):**
```css
.warpOverlay {
    position: absolute;
    inset: 0;
    z-index: 10;
    pointer-events: none;
    background: radial-gradient(
        ellipse at center,
        transparent 20%,
        rgba(95, 146, 255, 0.15) 60%,
        rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6) 100%
    );
    animation: warpPulse 1.8s ease-in-out forwards;
}

@keyframes warpPulse {
    0%   { opacity: 0; }
    15%  { opacity: 1; }
    85%  { opacity: 1; }
    100% { opacity: 0; }
}
```

**Overlay Pattern Examples:**
- `.previewPanel` (lines 115-130): Bottom-right glass-morphism with blur
- `.galaxyPreviewPanel` (lines 294-308): Similar pattern for galaxy info
- All overlays use: `backdrop-filter: blur()`, `border: 1px solid rgba(...)`
- Z-index hierarchy: overlays at 1010, containers at 1000, warp at 10

---

## Key Patterns for "Constellation Focus" Implementation

1. **State Management**: Add `focusedConstellationId` state in Universe.jsx
2. **Canvas Rendering**: Modify ConstellationLayer's `sceneFunc` to check if a constellation is focused and apply enhanced glow
3. **Star Highlighting**: Pass `focusedStarIds` to StarGroup to trigger enhanced glow on the two connected stars
4. **Dim Effect**: Either use a full-screen overlay with opacity, or conditionally modify opacity in canvas rendering
5. **ESC Handler**: Follow existing pattern (lines 629-637) to cancel focus
6. **Click Handler**: Add click detection on constellation lines — requires `listening={true}` on the Shape or a separate interactive layer for constellation lines
7. **Overlay Style**: Create a `.constellationFocusOverlay` CSS class following the warp overlay pattern

---

This is comprehensive information for implementing the feature following the existing patterns and architecture.

lines: -# Constellation Enhancements: Star Indicators, Glow Lines, Travel Buttons
-1. Stars in constellations have no visual indicator — users can't tell which stars are linked
-2. Constellation lines look like simple dashed lines, not like ethereal night-sky constellations
-3. You can only "wormhole" to semantically-linked stars, but not to constellation-linked stars
-## Feature 1: Constellation Ring Indicator on Stars
-Add a subtle dashed ring around stars that participate in accepted constellations, colored by their chain color.
-Accept `constellationColor` prop. Add between outer atmosphere and inner glow:
-Replace current Konva `Line` components with a single Canvas2D `Shape` using triple-layer glow rendering.
-Replace the current multi-Line approach with a `Shape` using `sceneFunc`:
-**Accepted line draw function (3 layers per line):**
-- Layer 1: Wide underglow — solid, `strokeWidth: 8`, opacity `0.06`, `shadowBlur: 20`, `shadowColor` at `0.3` opacity
-- Layer 3: Thin animated core — dashed `[14, 10]`, `strokeWidth: 1.2`, opacity `0.45`, animated `lineDashOffset` at `time * 0.04`
-**Labels:** Keep as Konva `<Text>` nodes (need `scaleX/scaleY` zoom correction)
-## Feature 3: Constellation Travel Buttons in Preview Panel
-`server/sql/constellations.sql` — add `a_title TEXT, b_title TEXT` to return type:
--- Add ja.title AS a_title, jb.title AS b_title to SELECT
-Titles come from the already-joined `journals ja/jb` tables — zero extra cost.
-            links.push({ constellation: c, targetStarId: c.star_id_b, targetTitle: c.b_title || 'Unnamed Star', label: c.label });
-            links.push({ constellation: c, targetStarId: c.star_id_a, targetTitle: c.a_title || 'Unnamed Star', label: c.label });
-- Fall back to constellation's raw `a_universe_x/y` or `b_universe_x/y`
-Purple-accented button (`#b05fff`) to distinguish from wormhole buttons (blue `#5f92ff`):
-| `client/src/components/Universe/layers/ConstellationLayer.jsx` | Rewrite: Canvas2D Shape with triple-layer glow |
-| `client/src/components/Universe/Universe.jsx` | Add `constellationStarColors` memo, `getConstellationLinksForPost`, `handleConstellationTravel`, travel buttons in preview panel |
-1. **Star indicator**: Stars in accepted constellations show a subtle dashed ring in their chain color; non-constellation stars unchanged
-2. **Glow lines**: Accepted constellation lines render with soft shadowBlur glow + animated dash core; look ethereal not mechanical
-3. **Pending lines**: Still faint and dashed for own pending constellations
-4. **Travel buttons**: Click a constellation star → preview panel shows purple "Fly to [title]" button for each linked star → clicking flies camera there
-5. **Far-away travel**: Constellation travel works even when target star isn't loaded in viewport (uses fallback RPC positions)
-6. **Wormhole buttons**: Still show independently (blue) alongside constellation buttons (purple)
+# Constellation Focus: Click Line → Spotlight Stars, Dim Universe

+When a user clicks a constellation line, the two connected stars should glow intensely while the rest of the universe dims into darkness. This creates a dramatic "spotlight" effect that highlights the relationship between linked stars.
+- Skip focus if in link mode
+**ESC handler:** Extend existing ESC useEffect pattern to also dismiss constellation focus.
+- z-index 5 (below UI at 1010, but provides click-to-dismiss area)
+- Dark semi-transparent background with subtle blur
+**Add `Line` import** back from `react-konva` (for hit detection only).
+**Remove `listening={false}` from Layer.** Keep it on Shape and Text.
+**Add hit detection Lines** after the labels (accepted lines only):
+- Focused line: boost opacity (underglow 0.15, body 0.3, core 0.7) + larger shadowBlur (30, 14)
+- Non-focused lines: heavily dimmed (opacity ~0.02) — nearly invisible
+**Dim other elements when focused:** Galaxy labels, bridges, and galaxy visuals get reduced opacity when `focusedStarIds` is set (wrap their rendering shapes with conditional opacity).
+**When `isDimmed`:** Set Group opacity to `0.06` — stars nearly vanish.
+- Add extra wide glow circle: `radius={r * 5}`, radial gradient, very soft
+| `Universe.jsx` | `focusedConstellation` state, derived `focusedStarIds`, focus/dismiss handlers, ESC key, overlay div, pass props |
+| `ConstellationLayer.jsx` | Import `Line`, add hit detection lines, focus-aware rendering in sceneFunc, remove `listening={false}` from Layer |
+| `InteractiveLayer.jsx` | Accept `focusedStarIds`, compute `isFocused`/`isDimmed` per star, dim other elements |
+| `StarGroup.jsx` | Accept `isFocused`/`isDimmed`, enhanced glow when focused, reduced opacity when dimmed |
+1. Click an accepted constellation line → two stars glow intensely, everything else dims
+2. Press ESC or click the dark overlay → focus dismissed, universe returns to normal
+4. Link mode is not affected (focus clicks ignored during link mode)
+5. Clicking a star while not in focus mode still works normally (star selection)
